title: Kentwood Bed
summary: A panel-and-slat headboard and footboard bed on 2x8 siderails — plywood panels trimmed in 1x4/1x3 with a horizontal slat pattern, compatible with a slat system or a standard box spring.
description:
This bed's headboard and footboard share one panel design at two heights: a plywood panel trimmed in 1x4 around the edges and 1x3 slats laid horizontally across the face, with the footboard simply using shorter legs than the headboard. Drill the pocket holes for attaching legs and the top trim into both panels early, before either panel is fully trimmed out — those holes get buried under the slats otherwise and are much harder to add later.

The siderail cleats are genuinely flexible on placement, and the plan calls this out directly: if you're using a box spring, mount the cleat at the lowest position the siderail allows; if you're going with a slat system instead, mount it near the top — 1" down from the siderail's top edge — since a slat system needs less clearance underneath than a box spring does.

**Consider bed rail hardware instead of screws for the siderail-to-post connection** if you'll ever need to break the bed down to move it — screwed rails hold better long-term, but hardware brackets are what let a bed frame come apart for a doorway or a move without re-drilling holes. Either way, attach the slats or bunkie board last, once the whole frame is square and true.

tools.note: Cutting the plywood panels and ripping slats.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Drill the panel pocket holes
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around the side and top edges of both plywood panels — these will attach the legs and top trim in later steps.

steps.title: Add the panel trim
steps.body: Attach the top and bottom trim to the fronts of both panels.

steps.title: Attach the panel slats
steps.body: Attach the horizontal slats across each panel, spaced evenly.

steps.title: Attach the headboard legs
steps.body: Attach the longer legs to the headboard panel through the predrilled pocket holes.

steps.title: Attach the footboard legs
steps.body: Attach the shorter legs to the footboard panel the same way.

steps.title: Build the siderails
steps.body: Attach the cleats to the siderails — at the lowest position if using a box spring, or 1" down from the top edge if using a slat system. Attach the siderails to the headboard and footboard legs, using bed rail hardware instead of screws if you'll need to disassemble the bed later.

steps.title: Add the slats and finish
steps.body: Add the slats (or a bunkie board, or box spring) across the siderail cleats. Sand and finish the entire bed.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Bed with slatted panel headboard and footboard on 2x8 siderails
